title: 大家疲倦之際 不如睇下當年美國黑人點樣抗爭

replies.msg:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Montgomery_bus_boycott

當年佢地抗爭無乜花臣
只係拒絕乘坐黑人隔離巴士
萬眾一心 籌旗共渡難關
直至勝利為止 歷時382日

香港至今只經歷4個月
可能大家都感到寸步難行
但係同舟共濟 有錢出錢 有力出力
眾志成城 堅持到底 永不放棄
前方必定有希望

我支持大家玩杯葛
千祈唔好犯法

雨傘運動嗰陣 大家反思過 和平大愛 work 唔 work
好多歷史事件話比我地聽 唔係唔 work
不過有條件
當然你會問今時今日對住 CCP work 唔 work
我只可以同大家講
我地從來未試過團結一致將和平抗爭發揮至極限
萬眾一心的抗爭係從未出現過
不要小看每分力的威力

On Saturday, December 3, it was evident that the black community would support the boycott, and very few blacks rode the buses that day. On December 5, a mass meeting was held at the Holt Street Baptist Church to determine if the protest would continue.[30] Given twenty minutes notice, King gave a speech[31] asking for a bus boycott and attendees enthusiastically agreed. Starting December 7, Hoover's FBI noted the "agitation among negroes" and tried to find "derogatory information" about King.[32]

The boycott proved extremely effective, with enough riders lost to the city transit system to cause serious economic distress. Martin Luther King later wrote "[a] miracle had taken place." Instead of riding buses, boycotters organized a system of carpools, with car owners volunteering their vehicles or themselves driving people to various destinations. Some white housewives also drove their black domestic servants to work. When the city pressured local insurance companies to stop insuring cars used in the carpools, the boycott leaders arranged policies at Lloyd's of London.[33]
人人為我 我為人人
